content: Management focuses on improving functioning rather than symptom elimination. Regular scheduled visits with single provider reduce unnecessary testing/procedures. Validate patient experience while gently redirecting from organic focus. CBT addresses catastrophic thinking, reduces healthcare utilization, improves coping. Mindfulness-based therapies help with symptom acceptance. Antidepressants (SSRIs/SNRIs) may help comorbid depression/anxiety and have some evidence for reducing somatic symptom burden. For Conversion Disorder: physical therapy, occupational therapy, psychological treatment addressing stressors. Avoid reinforcing sick role while maintaining therapeutic alliance. Gradual activity increase, focus on function over symptoms. Multidisciplinary approach optimal for complex cases.
